accurate.why: All claims accurately reflect the bill text across all three titles.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; statement that Subtitle A takes effect 18 months after enactment and filter bubble provision one year after enactment are correct.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's primary purposes across all three titles.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Accurately describes amendments to COPPA (Title II) and Title 31 (Title III) and new duties under Title I.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes key exceptions to covered platform definition and duty of care limitations.

effective_dates.why: Notes 18-month effective date for Subtitle A, 1-year for Subtitle B, and 180-day Kids Online Safety Council deadline.

major_provisions_covered.why: Thoroughly covers all three titles including duty of care, safeguards, COPPA extension to teens, filter bubble transparency, and reports elimination.

correct_entities.why: FTC, COPPA, National Academy of Sciences all correctly referenced.

correct_figures.why: 10 million monthly user threshold, 18-month and 1-year effective dates, 180-day council deadline all correctly stated.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
